archimate:Representation.name: RDF Serialisations
archimate:Representation.documentation:
Definition: RDF serialization can be defined as the process of encoding RDF (Resource Description Framework) data into a specific format or syntax that enables the storage, transmission, and exchange of structured data in a machine-readable manner. It involves transforming RDF triples into a serialized form, allowing data to be easily parsed, interpreted, and processed by both humans and computer systems.
Source: W3C
Source reference: https://www.w3.org/TR/rdf11-primer/
LOST view: SV-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/RDFSerialisationsRepresentation
ABB name: egovera:RDFSerialisationsRepresentation
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/RDFSerialisationsRepresentation
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataRepresentation

properties.value: RDF serialization can be defined as the process of encoding RDF (Resource Description Framework) data into a specific format or syntax that enables the storage, transmission, and exchange of structured data in a machine-readable manner. It involves transforming RDF triples into a serialized form, allowing data to be easily parsed, interpreted, and processed by both humans and computer systems.
